title: Three-stage template expansion with shell safety
purpose: Skill templates contain dynamic placeholders that must be resolved before the model sees the content. This brief implements the three-stage expansion pipeline with full shell execution safety: global disable policy, cwd specification, stdout cap, stderr handling. This is what makes Claude Code SKILL.md files work in Norn without modification.
task: Create skill/template.rs with an expand() function that processes a skill body through three sequential stages. Stage 1 executes backtick-bang commands with safety controls (disable policy, cwd, 32KB stdout cap, 1KB stderr in failures, 5s timeout). Stage 2 resolves dollar-sign arguments and built-ins. Stage 3 resolves mustache variables. Each stage is single-pass; replacement text is visible to subsequent stages.

requirements.title: Stage 1: Inline backtick-bang execution
requirements.spec: WHEN the skill body contains !`command` patterns, stage 1 SHALL execute each command via sh -c (or the configured shell), replace the pattern with trimmed stdout, and enforce a 5-second timeout.
acceptance: !`echo hello` is replaced with 'hello'
Multiple !`...` patterns in the same body are each independently executed
Text between backtick-bang patterns is preserved verbatim

requirements.title: Stage 1: Fenced block execution
requirements.spec: WHEN the skill body contains ```! fenced blocks, stage 1 SHALL execute the block content as a single shell invocation and replace the entire fenced block with trimmed stdout. Standard ``` code blocks (without !) SHALL NOT be executed.
acceptance: A ```! block containing 'echo one\necho two' produces 'one\ntwo'
Standard ``` code blocks without ! are left unchanged
The opening ```! and closing ``` are both removed from output

requirements.title: Stage 1: Failure markers with stderr
requirements.spec: IF a command fails (non-zero exit, timeout, spawn error), stage 1 SHALL replace the pattern with '[skill shell command failed: {error}]' including the first 1KB of stderr on failure.
acceptance: !`exit 1` produces a failure marker containing the exit code
!`sleep 10` with 5s timeout produces a timeout failure marker
Stderr content (up to 1KB) is included in the failure marker for non-zero exit

requirements.title: Stage 1: Shell field, cwd, disable policy, stdout cap
requirements.spec: Stage 1 SHALL respect the shell parameter (Bash vs PowerShell), run commands from the agent's cwd (not skill dir), respect disableSkillShellExecution by replacing with policy marker, and truncate stdout at 32KB.
acceptance: With shell=PowerShell, commands execute via pwsh -c
Commands run from the agent's working directory, not the skill directory
With disableSkillShellExecution=true, all commands produce '[shell command execution disabled by policy]'
Output exceeding 32KB is truncated with '[truncated — output exceeded 32KB]' appended

requirements.title: Stage 2: Dollar-sign expansion
requirements.spec: Stage 2 SHALL resolve $ARGUMENTS, $N/$ARGUMENTS[N], named $name, ${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}, ${CLAUDE_EFFORT}, ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}. $$ SHALL escape to literal $. Unrecognized $name references SHALL be left as-is.
acceptance: $ARGUMENTS resolves to the full arguments string
$0 resolves to the first positional argument
Named $issue with arguments=['issue'] resolves correctly
${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID} resolves to a non-empty session ID
$$ produces a literal $
$unknown_ref passes through as-is

requirements.title: Stage 3: Mustache variable expansion
requirements.spec: Stage 3 SHALL resolve {{name}} via the VariableStore's expand() function.
acceptance: {{session_id}} resolves via VariableStore
{{working_dir}} resolves to the current working directory
Text with no {{}} patterns passes through unchanged

requirements.title: Three-stage ordering and rescanning
requirements.spec: The three stages SHALL run in order: backtick-bang, dollar-sign, mustache. Each stage is single-pass. Replacement text produced by stage N is visible to stage N+1.
acceptance: Shell output containing $ARGUMENTS is dollar-expanded in stage 2
Dollar expansion output containing {{name}} is mustache-expanded in stage 3
The function signature accepts the shell setting, arguments, variable store, and built-in values

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement argument parsing from the SkillTool input — that is NS-004
SHALL NOT implement the $ARGUMENTS auto-append behavior — that is NS-004
verification: cargo test -p norn -- skill::template passes all new tests
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
Standard markdown code blocks in test input are not executed
Test with disableSkillShellExecution=true confirms all commands produce policy marker
